summary: Defense Minister Israel Katz stated the IDF will not withdraw from southern Lebanon until Hezbollah is disarmed, noting near-total destruction of Shiite villages along the line of contact and that the Lebanese army refused to enter the Ali Taher Ridge tunnel area. IDF forces killed Ismail al-Masri, the military security chief of Hamas's Rafah Brigade, and eliminated a Hezbollah militant in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon, during which Sgt. David Hazut was killed. Syria's President Ahmed al-Sharaa condemned Israeli actions, declaring Syria would not be a testing ground for foreign conspiracies, while Qatar suspended all maritime activities citing public safety.
key_points: Defense Minister Katz said the IDF will not withdraw a millimeter from Lebanon until Hezbollah is disarmed, with near-100% destruction of Shiite villages in western/central southern Lebanon and 73% in the eastern sector. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)
IDF and Shin Bet killed Ismail al-Masri, military security chief of Hamas's Rafah Brigade, who was responsible for restoring Hamas's military capabilities in the sector. (Source: @idf_telegram/24471)

IDF Golani Brigade troops killed a Hezbollah militant in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon, during a search operation; Sgt. David Hazut was killed and another soldier lightly wounded in the clash. (Source: @New_security8200/202136)

Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa condemned Israel, stating Syria is not a testing ground for foreign conspiracies and will not accept the realization of others' ambitions at the cost of Syrian blood. (Source: @SharghDaily/168778)

Qatar's Ministry of Transport suspended all maritime activities nationwide, including recreational boats, fishing boats, and jet skis, until further notice for public safety. (Source: @SharghDaily/168776)
The Wall Street Journal reported that planned US-Iran talks in Switzerland this week were halted at the last minute due to the renewal of fighting. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93294)

Iran's Assembly of Experts saw 63 of 88 members sign a statement calling killing of Trump and Netanyahu a religious duty, though the secretariat later called the statement irregular. (Source: @VahidOnline/76733)
French President Macron claimed France decided to work with Oman and other partners toward mining the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @SharghDaily/168784)

sections.executive_summary: Defense Minister Israel Katz declared the IDF will not withdraw from southern Lebanon until Hezbollah is disarmed, citing near-total destruction of Shiite border villages and the Lebanese army's refusal to clear Hezbollah tunnels at Ali Taher Ridge. The IDF conducted targeted killings of senior Hamas and Hezbollah operatives in Gaza and southern Lebanon, suffering one fatality in the Lebanon operation. Regional tensions escalated as Syria's president condemned Israeli actions, Qatar suspended maritime activities, and planned US-Iran talks in Switzerland were cancelled due to renewed fighting.

points: The Wall Street Journal reported that planned US-Iran negotiations in Switzerland this week were halted at the last minute due to the renewal of fighting between Israel and Iran/Hezbollah. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93294)

63 of 88 members of Iran's Assembly of Experts signed a statement resembling a fatwa calling the killing of US President Trump and PM Netanyahu a religious duty for devout Muslims, though the secretariat later described the statement as irregular. (Source: @VahidOnline/76733)

Defense Minister Katz stated that if Iran attacks Israel with ballistic missiles in response to actions in Lebanon, the IDF will respond independently, and that this could happen within two days. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)
French President Macron claimed France decided to cooperate with Oman and other partners toward mining the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @SharghDaily/168784)
The US Ambassador to the UN stated that Trump's patience with Iran is beginning to run out. (Source: @pkpoi/143022)

Defense Minister Katz stated Israel has no territorial ambitions in Lebanon but the IDF will not withdraw until Hezbollah is disarmed, and that this position is accepted by the US and anchored in the military annex to the Israel-Lebanon framework agreement. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)

Katz confirmed the IDF will not withdraw from three security zones in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza, and described the IDF presence in Lebanon as long-term, doubting the Lebanese army would confront Hezbollah. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)

Katz stated the IDF still has more tunnels to destroy, including a major Hezbollah underground system beneath Beaufort Ridge, which he said will be destroyed with 500 tons of explosives. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)

IDF and Shin Bet killed Ismail al-Masri, military security chief of Hamas's Rafah Brigade, who was working to restore Hamas's military security and intelligence capabilities in the sector. (Source: @idf_telegram/24471)

IDF Golani Brigade troops killed a Hezbollah militant in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon; Sgt. David Hazut was killed and another soldier lightly wounded during the operation. (Source: @New_security8200/202136)

The IDF confirmed it had tried to get the Lebanese army to enter the Ali Taher Ridge area, under which Hezbollah has a major tunnel system with approximately 30 operatives believed holed up, but the Lebanese army refused. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)

Chief of Staff Zamir marked 1,000 days of war, stating the conflict has changed operational methods and perceptions, and that the military is preparing for continued fighting and future challenges. (Source: @inon_yttach/9205)

IDF and Shin Bet killed Ismail al-Masri, the military security chief of Hamas's Rafah Brigade, who was a senior figure responsible for restoring Hamas's military security and intelligence mechanisms in the Rafah Brigade area. (Source: @idf_telegram/24471)

IDF Battalion 53 transitioned from two months of operational activity in southern Lebanon to operational deployment in the southern Rafah coastal area, conducting ongoing counterterrorism operations. (Source: @idf_telegram/24472)

IDF and Shin Bet also killed Zahir Barham Halil Abu Salim, an Islamic Jihad militant who infiltrated Israeli territory on October 7 and participated in kidnapping civilians from their homes and holding them captive, in northern Gaza. (Source: @inon_yttach/9204)

Defense Minister Katz stated there is almost 100 percent destruction in Shiite villages along the line of contact in the western and central sectors of southern Lebanon, and 73% destruction in the eastern sector. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)
Katz said approximately 600,000 Shiites from southern Lebanon villages have fled, and another 700,000 have been displaced from Hezbollah's Dahiyeh stronghold in Beirut. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)

IDF Golani Brigade troops killed a Hezbollah militant in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon, during a search operation; Sgt. David Hazut was killed and another soldier lightly wounded. (Source: @New_security8200/202136)

The Lebanese army refused the IDF's request to enter the Ali Taher Ridge area to clear Hezbollah operatives from a major tunnel system; the IDF remains stationed above ground while approximately 30 Hezbollah operatives are believed underground. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)
Several airstrikes struck southern Lebanon amid ongoing IDF operations north of the Litani River and expansion of the security zone. (Source: @shin_persian/7286)
An artillery bombardment targeted Kfar Tebnit in southern Lebanon. (Source: @redlinkleb/28998)
An attack was reported in Mifdun, southern Lebanon. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93297)

Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa condemned Israeli actions, stating that Israel has consistently targeted Syria's stability since the fall of the previous regime and is now trying to turn Syrian territory into endless chaos. (Source: @SharghDaily/168778)

Al-Sharaa declared that Syria is neither a testing ground for foreign conspiracies nor a place for others' ambitions to be realized at the cost of Syrian children's and women's blood. (Source: @SharghDaily/168778)
Al-Sharaa stated that Syrians throughout history have rejected division and are strong enough to overcome Israeli efforts to fragment the nation. (Source: @SharghDaily/168778)

Qatar's Ministry of Transport suspended all maritime activities nationwide until further notice for public safety, covering recreational boats, fishing boats, jet skis, and similar vessels. (Source: @SharghDaily/168776)
A Qatari citizen was killed from gunfire due to military actions in the area, according to the Qatari Foreign Ministry. (Source: @New_security8200/202137)
French President Macron claimed France decided to work with Oman and other partners toward mining the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @SharghDaily/168784)

key_developments: Defense Minister Katz declared the IDF will not withdraw from Lebanon until Hezbollah is disarmed, citing near-total destruction of Shiite border villages and describing the IDF presence as long-term. (Source: @manniefabian/58707)
IDF and Shin Bet killed Ismail al-Masri, military security chief of Hamas's Rafah Brigade, who was restoring Hamas's military capabilities in the sector. (Source: @idf_telegram/24471)
IDF Golani Brigade troops killed a Hezbollah militant in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon; Sgt. David Hazut was killed and another soldier lightly wounded. (Source: @New_security8200/202136)
Syrian President al-Sharaa condemned Israeli actions, declaring Syria would not be a testing ground for foreign conspiracies at the cost of Syrian blood. (Source: @SharghDaily/168778)
Qatar suspended all maritime activities nationwide until further notice for public safety, covering all types. (Source: @SharghDaily/168776)
Planned US-Iran talks in Switzerland were halted at the last minute due to renewed fighting, according to the Wall Street Journal. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93294)

63 of 88 members of Iran's Assembly of Experts signed a statement calling killing of Trump and Netanyahu a religious duty, though the secretariat later called it irregular. (Source: @VahidOnline/76733)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment is highly escalatory, with Defense Minister Katz explicitly warning that Iranian ballistic missile retaliation for Israeli actions in Lebanon could occur within two days and that the IDF has prepared targets in Iran. The IDF's expanded security zone north of the Litani River and continued tunnel destruction operations in southern Lebanon indicate sustained offensive operations despite the framework agreement. The Lebanese army's refusal to clear Hezbollah positions at Ali Taher Ridge suggests the disarmament precondition for IDF withdrawal remains unmet, making the IDF presence effectively indefinite. The cancellation of US-Iran talks removes a potential diplomatic off-ramp.
sections.regional_response: Syria's President al-Sharaa issued a strong condemnation of Israeli actions, framing them as an existential threat to Syrian unity and stability, while asserting Syrian resilience against fragmentation. Qatar's suspension of all maritime activities suggests heightened threat perception in the Gulf, possibly linked to Iranian military posturing around the Strait of Hormuz. France's reported willingness to participate in mining the Strait of Hormuz with Oman signals European concern over Iranian escalation. The US-Israel strategic linkage between the Lebanon and Iran arenas, as described by Katz, continues to shape operational constraints on Israeli military action.
sections.intelligence_notes: The near-total destruction of Shiite villages along the Lebanon-Israel border and displacement of approximately 1.3 million people (600,000 from southern Lebanon villages and 700,000 from Dahiyeh) represents a significant demographic transformation with long-term strategic implications. The IDF's stated plan to destroy the Beaufort Ridge tunnel system with 500 tons of explosives indicates the scale of Hezbollah's underground infrastructure. The internal split within Iran's Assembly of Experts—with the secretariat distancing itself from the fatwa-like statement signed by 63 members—suggests regime-level debate over the appropriate response to the of Khamenei. The IDF's rotation of Battalion 53 from Lebanon back to Rafah demonstrates continued operational tempo across multiple theaters.
